title: Internet-facing asset × unpatched critical CVE
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate internal hosts (covered by 22.43.1 in aggregate).
evidence: Saved search 'internet_facing_critical_cve' (hourly) intersects external-facing inventory with unpatched critical CVEs present on any such host.

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 risk-management measures include vulnerability handling on exposed assets.
PCI 6.3.3 requires critical vulnerabilities be addressed within one month; Internet-facing is the worst case.
RA-5 vulnerability scanning and remediation; Internet-facing is a priority.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Host web-01 is in the external-facing inventory and has CVE-2024-XXXX (CVSS 9.8) unpatched for 3 days; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Host is on internal network; no alert (different UC handles internal).

dataSources: Vulnerability scanner feeds, external-facing inventory (Shodan, Censys, AWS ALB/NLB, cloud LBs).
spl:
index=vuln earliest=-24h cvss_v3_base>=9.0
| lookup external_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT external_exposed
| where external_exposed="true"
| stats min(_time) as first_seen values(cve) as cves by host
| eval age_days=round((now()-first_seen)/86400,0)
| where age_days>0
| table host cves age_days
description: Any Internet-facing asset with an unpatched critical CVE is the single highest-priority audit and security finding; this UC keeps it at one-hour latency.
value: Eliminates the gap between scanner output and risk-based patch-SLA tracking.
implementation: (1) Maintain external_asset_inventory.csv refreshed daily from LB config or external attack-surface tooling; (2) run hourly; (3) SOAR auto-escalates on hit.
visualization: Table of Internet-facing hosts with critical CVEs, bar chart of patch-age buckets, single-value 'Internet-facing hosts fully patched'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application

knownFalsePositives: Honeypot / deliberately-exposed research assets can be excluded via an inventory annotation.
